setting.note: Twain renamed his boyhood Hannibal “St. Petersburg”, but built it exactly: the Mississippi bank, the cave, the graveyard on the hill, Cardiff Hill above the town. Hannibal has marked those spots for tourists ever since, which makes it a rare novel a reader can already walk. It brings Mark Twain and the Mississippi into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Mark Twain, first published 1876; the author died in 1910.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1876, well before 1929) and in the United Kingdom and EU, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1910).
rights.attribution: Mark Twain, The Adventures of Tom Sawyer (1876). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: the green valley shining in the sun
palette.note: The summer green of the river valley the boys look out on from the cave mouth, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

tom.name: Tom Sawyer
tom.colorSourceQuote: a thin blue breath of smoke
